surface.name: Klarna Payments playground web integration with the in-line widget
surface.selection_basis: Klarna's web prerequisites explicitly call the in-line widget the recommended solution and state that its guide covers initiation, checkout, and order creation. The official testing guide publicly documents playground account creation and key generation, allowing the full route to be reconstructed without assuming an existing merchant account.

alternatives_considered.name: Hosted Payment Page
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Klarna presents browser redirect as an alternative and explicitly recommends the in-line widget for the best user experience.

alternatives_considered.name: Platform integrations
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: These are platform-specific plugin routes rather than Klarna's general direct web-payment guide.

alternatives_considered.name: Mobile SDK
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is the strongly recommended route for mobile applications, not a website integration.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Klarna playground account, generate test API credentials, integrate the recommended web in-line widget, and complete one test payment until Klarna returns an order ID.
assumed_prior_state: No Klarna merchant account, Merchant portal access, merchant ID, API credential, payment session, checkout integration, authorization token, or order is assumed.
The developer has a web checkout and server capable of API requests and serving JavaScript.
The test uses Klarna's sample customer and payment data, not real personal data.
No signup, terms acceptance, credential generation, API request, or test purchase was performed during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Create a one-time payment order in the Klarna playground
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A test customer completes the Klarna widget flow and the server exchanges the authorization token for a Klarna order.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The create-order request succeeds, closes the payment session, charges the test customer, and returns `order_id`, `fraud_status`, and `authorized_payment_method`.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: A create-order success response containing `order_id`, `fraud_status`, and `authorized_payment_method`.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Klarna structures the direct web integration as initiate a payment, check out, and create an order. Order management is linked as the next section, so the first returned test order is the documented integration terminal.

prerequisites.requirement: A test Merchant portal account in the correct Klarna credential region.
Acceptance of Klarna's Terms & Conditions while activating the playground account.
A playground Klarna API Key ID, used as API username, and Secret, used as API password.
A supported purchase country, locale, currency, order amount, tax amount, and order lines for a one-time purchase.
A server-side API client and a checkout page that can load Klarna's JavaScript SDK.

primary_path.action: Open Klarna's Before you test guide.
primary_path.output: Playground prerequisites and account steps are shown.
primary_path.success_signal: The guide lists Merchant portal access, test API keys, and sample data.

primary_path.action: Click Log in in the Klarna Docs navigation.
primary_path.output: The credential region and environment selector opens.
primary_path.success_signal: Region and environment controls are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Use the region matching the intended API endpoints.

primary_path.action: Select the credential region, select Playground, and click Sign up.
primary_path.output: The playground account signup form opens.
primary_path.success_signal: An email field and SIGN-UP action are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A production environment would not create the test account used by this path.

primary_path.action: Enter an email address and click SIGN-UP.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Email delivery time is not stated.

primary_path.action: Open the confirmation email and click Activate account.
primary_path.output: Account activation continues in the browser.
primary_path.success_signal: Terms, password, and name setup are presented.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No expiry or resend path is documented on this page.

primary_path.action: Accept the Terms & Conditions, choose a new password, enter a name, and log in to the Merchant portal.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer is authenticated in the test Merchant portal.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Password requirements are not stated in the guide.

primary_path.action: Open Payment settings, select Klarna API Keys, and click Generate new Klarna API key.
primary_path.output: A downloadable API credential file is generated.
primary_path.success_signal: The key dialog offers the .txt file.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The guide does not document generation latency.

primary_path.action: Download the .txt file and close the dialog.
details: The secret is displayed only once.
primary_path.output: The developer has Key ID and Secret for playground API URLs.
primary_path.success_signal: Both credential values are stored securely.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the secret is lost, a new key is required.

primary_path.action: Create a one-time payment session with an authenticated POST to the regional playground `/payments/v1/sessions` endpoint.
details: Use HTTP Basic authentication with Key ID as username and Secret as password.
Set intent to buy and provide acquiring channel, purchase country, currency, locale, order amount, tax amount, and order lines.
primary_path.output: Klarna returns session_id, client_token, and payment_method_categories.
primary_path.success_signal: The response contains a client_token for the JavaScript SDK.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The session remains open for 48 hours or until an order is placed.

primary_path.action: Add Klarna's JavaScript SDK script and an asynchronous callback to the checkout page.
primary_path.output: Klarna.Payments becomes available in the callback.
The SDK is bound to the payment session.
primary_path.success_signal: Initialization completes before load is called.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The token depends on a session that expires after 48 hours.

primary_path.action: Add the `klarna-payments-container` element and call `Klarna.Payments.load` with its selector.
details: Use identifier, localized name, and asset_urls from the session response when presenting the option.
primary_path.output: The Klarna widget loads in checkout.
primary_path.success_signal: The callback returns `show_form: true` and the Klarna option is displayed.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If show_form is false, Klarna is unavailable for that purchase and the option must be handled accordingly.

primary_path.action: Select Klarna in the checkout and trigger the Buy action using Klarna's sample customer data.
details: Do not use real personal data in playground.
primary_path.success_signal: The test customer can log in and choose a Klarna payment option.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The test must use region-appropriate sample data.

primary_path.action: Call `Klarna.Payments.authorize` with the required customer details when the Buy action occurs.
details: Provide billing address and, when distinct, shipping address.
Avoid delays between the click and authorize call so the browser does not block Klarna's pop-up.
primary_path.output: Klarna evaluates the purchase and returns an authorization token on success.
primary_path.success_signal: The authorize response contains an authorization_token.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An authorization failure or browser-blocked pop-up stops order creation.

primary_path.action: Send the authorization token in an authenticated POST to the session's `/order` endpoint within 60 minutes.
details: Use the regional playground API URL and the same HTTP Basic credentials.
primary_path.output: Klarna creates the one-time payment order and closes the session.
primary_path.success_signal: The response returns order_id, fraud_status, and authorized_payment_method.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Validation can fail if order creation occurs more than 60 minutes after authorization or request data is invalid.

primary_path.action: Verify that the create-order success response contains `order_id`, `fraud_status`, and `authorized_payment_method`.
primary_path.output: The first playground Klarna order is confirmed.
primary_path.success_signal: The documented example shows `fraud_status: ACCEPTED`, but the success contract returns the fraud-status field rather than guaranteeing that example value for every order.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Use Merchant portal logs for API troubleshooting if the request fails.

branches.path: Use Klarna Hosted Payment Page instead of the in-line JavaScript widget.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces client-side SDK integration with a server-side HPP session and customer redirect.

branches.path: Use intent tokenize, buy_and_tokenize, or buy_and_default_tokenize as documented for the recurring scenario.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes session intent, authorization response, and order or token terminal.

branches.path: Hide or disable Klarna, or leave it visible with Klarna's unavailable message.
branches.effect_on_first_success: No Klarna order can be created for that test purchase; use valid supported test data before retrying.

friction_gates.description: The developer must choose the credential region and Playground environment.
A verified playground Merchant portal account is required.
Klarna requires Terms & Conditions acceptance during account activation.
The API Secret is only displayed in the generated file once.
Klarna pre-assesses availability and only displays the payment option when load returns show_form true.
The order must be created within 60 minutes of authorization.

sources.title: Set up Klarna's JavaScript SDK
sections_used: Add a container to your page

sources.title: One-time payment order
evidence_supported: order_id, fraud_status, and authorized_payment_method response fields

excluded_after_success: Order capture, shipping, refunds, disputes, settlement, production contracts, and production credentials occur after the first playground order.
On-site messaging, Express Checkout, Sign in with Klarna, mobile SDK integration, subscriptions, and HPP customization are outside the selected first path.
